title: pilosella tardiuscula subsp. microvasconica
intro: Pilosella tardiuscula subsp. microvasconica, a rare and endemic subspecies of the Pilosella genus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Pilosella tardiuscula subsp. microvasconica include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or as a tincture, and are often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
